steps_list_form_data_entry_page_element_id_830605__text

Step 1: Get the Oven Ready the day before by heating oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Put a deep roasting tin onto the bottom shelf of the oven, then fill two-thirds full with boiling water. This creates a steam bath for the pudding. Make sure there’s another shelf directly above it for the Jam Roly-Poly to sit on. Tear off a large sheet of foil and greaseproof paper (about 30 x 40cm). Sit the greaseproof on top of the foil and butter it.
Step 2: Make the Suet Dough In a food processor, combine butter, self-raising flour, and vanilla seeds. Pulse until the butter has disappeared into the flour mixture. Transfer this mixture to a mixing bowl. Stir in the suet. Gradually pour in the milk, mixing with a cutlery knife until a sticky dough forms. You might need a drop more milk, depending on your flour, to achieve the right consistency.
Step 3: Assemble and Roll out the Roly-Poly Lightly flour a clean work surface. Turn the dough out onto the floured surface and quickly pat it together to smooth it. Roll the dough out into a square roughly 25 x 25cm. Evenly spread the raspberry jam all over the dough, making sure to leave a small gap along one edge (this will be your sealing edge).
Step 4: Roll and Seal Begin rolling the dough tightly from the edge opposite the jam-free gap. Once fully rolled, pinch the jam-free edge into the dough where it meets to seal the roll. Pinch the ends roughly as well to contain the jam. Carefully lift the roly-poly onto the greased paper, ensuring the seam is face down. Loosely bring up the greaseproof paper and foil around the pudding. Scrunch the edges and ends together to seal the parcel, but do not wrap it too tightly as the roly-poly will expand during cooking.
Step 5: Steam and Serve Carefully lift the sealed parcel directly onto the rack positioned above the roasting tin with boiling water. Cook in the preheated oven for 1 hour. Once cooked, remove from the oven and let the pudding sit for 5 minutes before unwrapping. Carefully open the foil and paper, then thickly slice the Jam Roly-Poly to serve, optionally with custard.

recipe.instructions:
Put a deep roasting tin onto the bottom shelf of the oven, and make sure that there’s another shelf directly above it. Pull the roasting tin out on its shelf, fill two-thirds with boiling water from the kettle, then carefully slide it back in. Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Tear off a large sheet of foil and greaseproof paper (about 30 x 40cm). Sit the greaseproof on top of the foil and butter it.
Tip butter, flour and vanilla seeds into a food processor; pulse until the butter has disappeared. Tip into a mixing bowl. Stir through the suet, pour in the milk and work together with a cutlery knife until you get a sticky dough. You may need a drop more milk, depending on your flour.
Tip the dough out onto a floured surface, quickly pat together to smooth, then roll out to a square roughly 25 x 25cm. Spread the jam all over, leaving a gap along one edge, then roll up from the opposite edge. Pinch the jam-free edge into the dough where it meets, and pinch the ends roughly, too. Carefully lift onto the greased paper, join-side down (you might find a flat baking sheet helpful for this), loosely bring up the paper and foil around it, then scrunch together along the edges and ends to seal. The roly-poly will puff quite a bit during cooking so don’t wrap it tightly. Lift the parcel directly onto the rack above the tin and cook for 1 hr.
Let the pudding sit for 5 mins before unwrapping, then carefully open the foil and paper, and thickly slice to serve.

Pull the roasting tin out on its shelf, fill two-thirds with boiling water from the kettle, then carefully slide it back in. Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Tear off a large sheet of foil and greaseproof paper (about 30 x 40cm). Sit the greaseproof on top of the foil and butter it.
Tip butter, flour and vanilla seeds into a food processor; pulse until the butter has disappeared. Tip into a mixing bowl. Stir through the suet, pour in the milk and work together with a cutlery knife until you get a sticky dough. You may need a drop more milk, depending on your flour.
Tip the dough out onto a floured surface, quickly pat together to smooth, then roll out to a square roughly 25 x 25cm. Spread the jam all over, leaving a gap along one edge, then roll up from the opposite edge. Pinch the jam-free edge into the dough where it meets, and pinch the ends roughly, too. Carefully lift onto the greased paper, join-side down (you might find a flat baking sheet helpful for this), loosely bring up the paper and foil around it, then scrunch together along the edges and ends to seal. The roly-poly will puff quite a bit during cooking so don’t wrap it tightly. Lift the parcel directly onto the rack above the tin and cook for 1 hr.
Let the pudding sit for 5 mins before unwrapping, then carefully open the foil and paper, and thickly slice to serve.

steps_list_form_data_entry_page_element_id_830605__text

Step 1: Get the Oven Ready the day before by heating oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Put a deep roasting tin onto the bottom shelf of the oven, then fill two-thirds full with boiling water. This creates a steam bath for the pudding. Make sure there’s another shelf directly above it for the Jam Roly-Poly to sit on. Tear off a large sheet of foil and greaseproof paper (about 30 x 40cm). Sit the greaseproof on top of the foil and butter it.
Step 2: Make the Suet Dough In a food processor, combine butter, self-raising flour, and vanilla seeds. Pulse until the butter has disappeared into the flour mixture. Transfer this mixture to a mixing bowl. Stir in the suet. Gradually pour in the milk, mixing with a cutlery knife until a sticky dough forms. You might need a drop more milk, depending on your flour, to achieve the right consistency.
Step 3: Assemble and Roll out the Roly-Poly Lightly flour a clean work surface. Turn the dough out onto the floured surface and quickly pat it together to smooth it. Roll the dough out into a square roughly 25 x 25cm. Evenly spread the raspberry jam all over the dough, making sure to leave a small gap along one edge (this will be your sealing edge).
Step 4: Roll and Seal Begin rolling the dough tightly from the edge opposite the jam-free gap. Once fully rolled, pinch the jam-free edge into the dough where it meets to seal the roll. Pinch the ends roughly as well to contain the jam. Carefully lift the roly-poly onto the greased paper, ensuring the seam is face down. Loosely bring up the greaseproof paper and foil around the pudding. Scrunch the edges and ends together to seal the parcel, but do not wrap it too tightly as the roly-poly will expand during cooking.
Step 5: Steam and Serve Carefully lift the sealed parcel directly onto the rack positioned above the roasting tin with boiling water. Cook in the preheated oven for 1 hour. Once cooked, remove from the oven and let the pudding sit for 5 minutes before unwrapping. Carefully open the foil and paper, then thickly slice the Jam Roly-Poly to serve, optionally with custard.

recipe.instructions:
Put a deep roasting tin onto the bottom shelf of the oven, and make sure that there’s another shelf directly above it. Pull the roasting tin out on its shelf, fill two-thirds with boiling water from the kettle, then carefully slide it back in. Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Tear off a large sheet of foil and greaseproof paper (about 30 x 40cm). Sit the greaseproof on top of the foil and butter it.
Tip butter, flour and vanilla seeds into a food processor; pulse until the butter has disappeared. Tip into a mixing bowl. Stir through the suet, pour in the milk and work together with a cutlery knife until you get a sticky dough. You may need a drop more milk, depending on your flour.
Tip the dough out onto a floured surface, quickly pat together to smooth, then roll out to a square roughly 25 x 25cm. Spread the jam all over, leaving a gap along one edge, then roll up from the opposite edge. Pinch the jam-free edge into the dough where it meets, and pinch the ends roughly, too. Carefully lift onto the greased paper, join-side down (you might find a flat baking sheet helpful for this), loosely bring up the paper and foil around it, then scrunch together along the edges and ends to seal. The roly-poly will puff quite a bit during cooking so don’t wrap it tightly. Lift the parcel directly onto the rack above the tin and cook for 1 hr.
Let the pudding sit for 5 mins before unwrapping, then carefully open the foil and paper, and thickly slice to serve.

steps_list_form_data_entry_page_element_id_830605__text

Step 1: Get the Oven Ready the day before by heating oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Put a deep roasting tin onto the bottom shelf of the oven, then fill two-thirds full with boiling water. This creates a steam bath for the pudding. Make sure there’s another shelf directly above it for the Jam Roly-Poly to sit on. Tear off a large sheet of foil and greaseproof paper (about 30 x 40cm). Sit the greaseproof on top of the foil and butter it.
Step 2: Make the Suet Dough In a food processor, combine butter, self-raising flour, and vanilla seeds. Pulse until the butter has disappeared into the flour mixture. Transfer this mixture to a mixing bowl. Stir in the suet. Gradually pour in the milk, mixing with a cutlery knife until a sticky dough forms. You might need a drop more milk, depending on your flour, to achieve the right consistency.
Step 3: Assemble and Roll out the Roly-Poly Lightly flour a clean work surface. Turn the dough out onto the floured surface and quickly pat it together to smooth it. Roll the dough out into a square roughly 25 x 25cm. Evenly spread the raspberry jam all over the dough, making sure to leave a small gap along one edge (this will be your sealing edge).
Step 4: Roll and Seal Begin rolling the dough tightly from the edge opposite the jam-free gap. Once fully rolled, pinch the jam-free edge into the dough where it meets to seal the roll. Pinch the ends roughly as well to contain the jam. Carefully lift the roly-poly onto the greased paper, ensuring the seam is face down. Loosely bring up the greaseproof paper and foil around the pudding. Scrunch the edges and ends together to seal the parcel, but do not wrap it too tightly as the roly-poly will expand during cooking.
Step 5: Steam and Serve Carefully lift the sealed parcel directly onto the rack positioned above the roasting tin with boiling water. Cook in the preheated oven for 1 hour. Once cooked, remove from the oven and let the pudding sit for 5 minutes before unwrapping. Carefully open the foil and paper, then thickly slice the Jam Roly-Poly to serve, optionally with custard.
